attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Defined in `build.sh` for these vector-capable CPUs:
- `nx27v` (line 80)
- `ax45mpv` (line 95)
- `ax45mpv_ace` (line 98)
- `a46mpv` (line 121)
- `ax46mpv` (line 124)
- `ax66` (line 130)

Then explicitly **undefined** (`-U`) by per-project `run.sh` overrides for `ax46mpv`, `a46mpv`, `ax66`, `nx27v`, `ax45mpv`, and re-defined (`-D`) only by `project/nx27v_qilai/run.sh:32`.

Net effect: among the project run scripts, only `nx27v_qilai` actually compiles with `ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD` defined. Other CPUs get it from `build.sh` defaults but their run.sh strips it.

content.text:
**Default (define):** `build.sh:95` for `ax45mpv`:
```
CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series ... -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD ..."
```

**Strip (undefine):** `project/ax45mpv/run.sh:56-58`:
```bash
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
```

So for `ax45mpv`, the macro stays defined unless `ENA_VDOT=0` (with vector ISA enabled), in which case `run.sh` undefines it.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app

content.text:
Yes. With `ax45mpv` defaults (`ENA_VEC_ISA=1`, `ENA_VDOT=1`):
- `build.sh:95` defines `ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD`
- `run.sh:56-58` strip branch does NOT fire (since `ENA_VDOT=1`)
- So the macro stays defined.

In `riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any.c`, vd4dot paths are gated by `ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD`:
- Line 54 branch: `__riscv_vd4dots_vv_i32m4_tu` intrinsic - requires `ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC` (NOT active, since `ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0`)
- Line 311 branch: inline-asm vd4dot - requires `ENA_TILING`
- Line 531 branch: inline-asm vd4dot via `NDS_VEC_V*` macros - requires `ENA_VEC_ELEN64` (active under `zve64d`/`VEC_CONFIG=4`)

So the **active path is line 531** -> uses **vd4dot** via inline assembly.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input
# $1: toolcahin prefix
# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
# $3: extra flags
################################################################################

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$1"
    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    set -x
        ${build_cmd}
        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # dump the disassembly
            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"

            echo "Start at $(date)"
            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            echo "End at $(date)"
        fi
    set +x

    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
        # the test program is executed.
        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            fail_list+=("${1}")
            echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log}
        else
            pass_num=$((pass_num + 1))
        fi

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            #dump inst/cycle into csv
            echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "inst" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "cycle count" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            #grep -w "miss stall" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $7}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            echo >> ${CSV_OUT}  #new line
        fi
    else
        fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi
}

################################################################################
#                                   main                                       #

#!/bin/bash
#set -x

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# This file is a board simulator wrapper shell script for both nds32le-elf & nds32be-elf,
# which is called during the gcc/plumhall/supertest testsuite verification flow.
#
# Currently, the nds32 simulator -- run binary on board -- does not run program in intuitive way.
# The simulatior requires a gdb expect file in which the execution file is strictly specified.
# We use this wrapper script to complete the work so that the testsuite can easily use it to do simulation.

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# check whether MYPC environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPC} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Warning! MYPC environment variable (where ICEman launches on) is not set yet."
    echo "GDB will take localhost as the target remote."
    echo
fi

# check whether MYPORT environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPORT} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Error! MYPORT environment variable is not set yet."
    echo "Please set MYPORT to the socket port number of ICEman for GDB connection."
    echo
    exit 1
fi

# the following variables will combine directory name and file name to form a full-path
RUN_GDB=$SIMULATOR_DIR/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# set gcc testsuite should be "yes" or "no", it will effect how to get return value.
GCC="no"

# set the board cache will be "on" or "lm". lm measns using local memory.
CACHE="on"

# set the board need reset-and-run or not.
RESET_BOARD="on"

# setup the PMA for HVM (it could be "on" or "off")
HVM_PMA="off"

# setup the DDR latency (it could be "on" or "off")
DDR_LATENCY="on"

# enable the non-blocking load (it could be "on" or "off")
NON_BLOCKING_LOAD="on"

# setup the D$ write-around threshold (it could be 0~3)
DC_WA_THRESHOLD="1"

# we should reset the board when we run first test case in every testsuite.
if [ ! -f "$SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done" ]; then
    RESET="on"
    touch $SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done
else
    RESET=$RESET_BOARD
fi

# physically invoke simulator
if test -r CVALSTDIN
then
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET $HVM_PMA $DDR_LATENCY $NON_BLOCKING_LOAD $DC_WA_THRESHOLD $CVALSTDIN
else
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET $HVM_PMA $DDR_LATENCY $NON_BLOCKING_LOAD $DC_WA_THRESHOLD
fi
# Note. Please do NOT add any command between expect $RUN_GDB blah blah blah... and ret=$? to make
# sure the scirpt can get correct return value from GDB.
ret=$?

cd ${OLD_PWD}

exit ${ret}

exit 1
fi

# compiler
if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
    CC="${3}"
else
    CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
fi

# customized optimization for 45-series
if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ mtune=andes-45-series ]]; then
    CFLAG+=" -DENA_45_SERIES_OPT"
fi

# extra compilation flags
if [ -n "${2}" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${2}"
fi

# output path
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
if [[ -n ${4} ]]; then
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${4}"
else
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}"
fi

#==================== extra optional flags ====================
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check the zfh extension could be enabled or not with
# the extra compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by the extra compilation flags.
# -----
# check the zfh extension could be enabled or not
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check the toolchain is [single|double]-abi or not
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series
        if [[ ${CPU} == nx27v ]] || [[ ${CPU} == *45* ]]; then
            CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

INCLUDE_CFLAG="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal -I${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include"
GLOBAL_CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${INCLUDE_CFLAG}"
RELEASE_FOLDER="${LIB_ROOT}/release"

# generate nds_version.c to make library version number
cd ${LIB_ROOT}
nds_autogen_info.sh "libnn"
if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
    mv ${LIB_ROOT}/nds_version.c "${LIB_ROOT}/Source"
fi

file.content:
#                                   main                                       #
################################################################################
ENA_TEST_ALL=1
ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=0
ENA_BASIC_TEST=0
ENA_CONCATE_TEST=0
ENA_CONV_TEST=0
ENA_FC_TEST=0
ENA_POOLING_TEST=0
ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST=0
ENA_UTIL_TEST=0

ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0           # only for the test programs with DYNAMIC allocation buffers
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0          # only run large test case to measure the performance (
                            # run the program with DYNAMIC allocation buffers)
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # grep instruction and cycle counts into csv
ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC=0   # run the test prgram with STATIC allocation buffers
ENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0          # The testers can turn on this switch as running the
                            # test programs in the environment not supporting
                            # FILE I/O, e.g. m4 or m7. The inputs will be randomly
                            # generated and the results will not be verified.
                            # (this switch is ONLY for STATIC allocation buffer tests)
ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER=0   # verify the wrapper header file between andes-nn and cmsis-nn

# Since strided load is not supported now. If vector extension
# is enabled, those functions use strided load won't be executed.
# if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mvh"* ]]; then
#     ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=1      #only run the test cases with unit stride acess
# else
    ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=0
# fi

#===== configure test environment_begin =====
CC=${1}
if [[ $CC =~ - ]]; then
    OBJDUMP="${CC%-*}-objdump"
else
    OBJDUMP="objdump"
fi
SIM_WRAPPER=${2}
EXTRA_FLAG="${3}"
CFLAGS="-Wall -Werror -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
MACRO="-DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=${ENA_RANDOM_INPUT}"
UNIT_TEST_PATH=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../unit_performance"
INC_DIR="-I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../Include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm-project/libhvm/include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include/"
LINKED_LIB="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libnn.a"
if [ ${ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC} -eq 1 ]; then
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_pf_"
else    #ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
